definition: Developed market food CPI weights vary significantly — Japan 26%, EU periphery 18-22%, UK ~16%, US ~13.5% — creating cross-country policy divergence risk from agricultural supply shocks. The BOJ vulnerability (26% food weight + yen weakness) connecting to monetary normalization risk is an underappreciated channel.
evidence: CPI weights are published facts: Japan 26%, EU periphery 18-22%, UK ~16%, US ~13.5%
BOJ vulnerability from 26% food weight + yen weakness is a valid and underappreciated channel
open_questions: Would the agricultural shock need to materialize before this becomes a tradeable dislocation?
What is the cross-DM rates dislocation magnitude from a significant food price shock?
How does this interact with BOJ normalization timing?
